title: Black Teens Called ‘Radioactive’ as D.C. Cuts Programs Meant to Help Them
description: As the City Council votes to advance a permanent youth curfew, advocates say the real problem isn’t being addressed.
author: Alecia Taylor

title: Virginia’s Redistricting Vote Was About Representation, Power, and History
description: After weeks of wall-to-wall campaigning, Virginians approved a new House map — but now it faces a legal hurdle.
author: Brandon Tensley

title: Atlanta’s ‘Cop City’ Makes a Black Neighborhood a Testing Lab for AI Policing
description: In America’s most surveilled city, a community loses forest land and gains a network of AI-enabled cameras.
author: Adam Mahoney, Madeline Thigpen

title: A Sudden Resignation Leaves Florida’s Blackest Congressional District in Limbo
description: U.S. Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick represented Florida’s 20th Congressional District since 2022 and stepped down amid an ethics investigation.
author: Brandon Tensley

title: In New Orleans, Black Cowboy Tradition Collides With Prison Rodeo Spectacle
description: As Angola’s prison rodeo debuts in the Superdome, Black riders in the city fight to preserve a tradition free from cages, cotton, and coerced labor.
author: Adam Mahoney

title: Trump’s Clash With the Pope Is Raising Alarm in Black Churches
description: “We serve a God who believes in peace and love, but you have an administration pushing war and hate,” a Chicago faith leader says.
author: Brandon Tensley

title: Justin Fairfax, Once a Rising Political Star, Kills Wife in Murder-Suicide
description: The investigation is ongoing, but authorities say the shooting seems to be related to a “complicated or messy divorce.”
author: Brandon Tensley

title: Railroad Wins Appeal to Take Generations-Old Land in Rural Georgia
description: Residents in Sparta have been fighting for years to protect their property from being seized through eminent domain.
author: Aallyah Wright

title: In Rural Mississippi, a Black Town Bets on New Homes to Build Wealth
description: In Jonestown, an all-Black town, elders are helping to build housing and youth activities, but want more community support.
author: Aallyah Wright

title: Residents Say Musk’s AI Supercomputer Is a ‘Death Sentence’ for Memphis Communities
description: A federal lawsuit claims xAI secretly fired up dozens of gas turbines, pumping smog and cancer‑linked chemicals into Black neighborhoods to fuel its growth.
author: Adam Mahoney

title: Michael Jackson’s Hometown Welcomes Film Screening Before World
description: Thousands gathered in in Gary, Indiana, for an emotional screening of a biopic of the music legend.
author: Rayonna Burton-Jernigan

title: Louisiana Might Eliminate This Black Elected Official’s Job Before He Can Start It
description: Calvin Duncan had been sentenced to life behind bars before he was exonerated and won a position that would allow him to tackle criminal justice reform.
author: Brandon Tensley

title: HBCUs Say They Stand to Lose Out if College Athlete Pay Rules Don’t Change
description: Grambling State University’s vice president of athletics urged Congress to create a national law as questions grow about the current system.
author: Alecia Taylor

title: Astronaut Victor Glover Jr. Returns From History-Making Mission to the Moon
description: The first Black astronaut to travel around the moon said his 10-day voyage is "about human history."
author: Capital B Staff

title: Black Service Members Warn Military Is Moving Backward
description: Military service has long been seen as a means of upward mobility for Black Americans, but recent actions raise questions about inclusion.
author: Brandon Tensley

title: Colorado Ranchers Fear For Their Lives After Shooting On Farm
description: After years of facing harassment, the Mallerys say they are still experiencing it.
author: Aallyah Wright

title: How to Find Black Farmers in Your Area and Support Them
description: Answers to your questions about how to shop local as the federal government continues to eliminate resources for Black farmers.
author: Aallyah Wright

title: In Roseland, Black Residents Were Told They Were Safe as Toxic Chemicals Spread
description: Newly released tests and documents in the Louisiana town contradict government reassurances and are fueling protest.
author: Adam Mahoney

title: Black Americans Warn Trump’s Threat to Iran Risks Dangerous Escalation
description: Some are calling the president’s comment a threat of “war crimes” and that “it’s time to use the 25th Amendment” to remove him from office.
author: Brandon Tensley, Adam Mahoney

title: Some Black Women Say the Best Thing They Did for Their Health Was Leave America
description: Germany’s universal health care system, though far from perfect, has become a refuge for those seeking care and stability.
author: Jenae Barnes

title: 2 Live Crew’s Uncle Luke Is Running for Congress and Calling Out Black Trump Supporters
description: The trailblazer of Southern rap has moved from pushing cultural boundaries and buttons to demanding political accountability.
author: Brandon Tensley

title: As USDA Cuts $300 Million, Black Farmers Say They Can’t Rely on the Government
description: The department nixed a Biden-era program meant to help underserved producers with access to land, capital, and markets.
author: Aallyah Wright

title: A Financial Crisis Could Upend Kentucky’s Only Public HBCU
description: A bill quickly moving through the Kentucky General Assembly would give the state a stake in the school's operations.
author: Alecia Taylor

title: The Supreme Court Fight That Could Unravel Who Gets to Be American
description: A case could upend a constitutional guarantee rooted in Reconstruction, raising concerns about rights long seen as settled.
author: Brandon Tensley

title: An Oil Explosion in a Black Texas Town Traces Back to Trump's Iran and Venezuela Crises
description: “The chickens have come home to roost,” one resident said. “Our exact fears have come true.”
author: Adam Mahoney

title: This Black Town Has E. Coli in Its Drinking Water, but Feds Just Cut Support
description: Cahokia Heights, ​a Black Illinois community, was already drowning in sewage. Now it faces the loss of crucial federal repair money.
author: Adam Mahoney

title: U.S. Votes No as UN Calls Slave Trade ‘Gravest Crime’ and Backs Reparations
description: Global leaders are moving toward reparations, but U.S. opposition leaves Black Americans fighting that battle largely on their own.
author: Adam Mahoney
